content:
Hi everyone,

I think this patchset is about able to go in. Could you please leave your Reviewed-by if you are okay with it?

I have not based it on the allocation API changes and I don't think it needs to be. It has one function that allocates and it does so by directly calling C and it takes gfp flags. If there are minor discrepancies between how the two patchsets pass such flags, then I'd rather fix it in the next cycle. Not basing it on the allocation API changes patchset ensures that it's simple for Matthew Wilcox to take the patch through his tree.

I will ask Matthew Wilcox to take the patch once I have a few more Reviewed-by tags.
what about the UserSliceReader::read_all function? That uses Vec and also allocates.
Also I think the flags from patch 4 could be using the allocation API flags
The flags from patch 4 are what I was talking about in my original message.
But if I have to fix read_all, then its a moot point.

For my own understanding, does the kernel do any level of locking? Like if you have one writer writing 10kB of 0x01, one writer writing the same 10kB to 0x02, and one reader all on different kthreads, would the reader see:

  1. Either fully 0x01 or 0x02
  2. Interleaved page-size (or smaller) blocks of 0x01 and 0x02
  3. A possibly random mix of both
To userspace? There's basically no guarantee whatsoever in that case.
Yeah, if a userspace program passes the same buffer to two syscalls in different thread, it's up to userspace for the consistency.
